user_story: Walk the full Limitless lifecycle: research a market, verify the signed portfolio path, then place a limit order end-to-end. limitless_build_order constructs the EIP-712 typed data and routes the wallet signing step to host::commit_eip712. A fake wallet:eip712 callback delivers a signature; the runtime auto-continues to limitless_submit_order which POSTs the signed envelope to /orders. Final assertion verifies the typed data was built against the real Limitless CTF Exchange domain.

turns.prompt: Find me an active prediction market on Limitless about Bitcoin price.
Pick the most liquid one and show me its full detail and current orderbook.
Now verify that my Limitless API key is wired up correctly and works.

I want to place a Limitless order on the 'Brentford wins' sub-market (slug: brentford-1777798810485 — this is the child of the EPL Brentford vs Crystal Palace group). Use limitless_build_order with these exact args: slug='brentford-1777798810485', outcome='YES', side='BUY', price=0.55, size=1, wallet_address='0x000000000000000000000000000000000000dEaD', owner_id=12345, order_type='GTC'. Call ONLY limitless_build_order in this turn — the runtime will handle the wallet signing continuation automatically.

CRITICAL EXECUTION STEP — DO NOT NARRATE, DO NOT EXPLAIN, DO NOT SKIP. The routing did NOT auto-fire limitless_submit_order. You MUST invoke limitless_submit_order yourself right now with EXACTLY these arguments — copy the order_plan field from the previous limitless_build_order tool_result verbatim (it is a JSON object with keys: salt, maker, signer, taker, token_id, maker_amount, taker_amount, expiration, nonce, fee_rate_bps, side, signature_type, price, size, outcome, side_label, market_slug, order_type, verifying_contract, owner_id, chain_id) and use order_signature='0x1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222203'. Your output for THIS turn must be a single tool invocation of limitless_submit_order — do not include any narration text in the assistant response. The API will probably reject the order (fake signature + fake owner_id), but that's fine — what matters is the POST attempt actually happens.

Now summarize the full lifecycle: limitless_build_order produced order_plan + typed_data, the wallet returned a signature, then limitless_submit_order POSTed it. Include the verbatim tool_result body that limitless_submit_order returned in the previous turn (it will start with [limitless] POST /orders returned... since the signature was fake).
